user_query:
I'm the engineering manager for our Core Platform team. Please read these files under input/ and generate a concise daily action digest for me in Department Lead mode, covering today and the next 72 hours: - input/calendar.json (events) - input/emails.csv (inbox highlights) - input/messages.jsonl (team/SMS/DMs) - input/weather.json (alerts/forecast) - input/tasks.md (working notes) - input/now.txt (ISO8601 current time for time-window checks)

Requirements:
1) Prioritize using the rules: P0 Must-do (payment/security/meeting <2h; time-sensitive msg needs reply today) max 3 items, P1 Should-do (24–72h meetings/prep/follow-ups/logistics) max 5, P2 Nice-to-have (low urgency) max 3. If uncertain, downgrade and mark as "(uncertain)".
2) Department Lead focus: team milestone risks, cross-team blockers, important stakeholder follow-ups, this-week delivery confidence. Include owner suggestions when possible (e.g., "Owner: <name/role>").
3) Each item must include: what happened, why it matters, and a deadline/time window.
4) Redact any secrets/tokens/account numbers (e.g., API keys, 16+ digit numbers). Do not invent data; prefer fewer high-confidence items.
5) Output both a human-friendly brief and a machine-readable version:
   - output/digest.md with clear sections: Must-do (today), Should-do (soon), Nice-to-have, Suggested next actions (1-line commands I can say next). Keep within the item count caps.
   - output/digest.json with the same content using this structure:
     {
       "must_do": [{"what": "...", "why": "...", "deadline": "..."}],
       "should_do": [ ... ],
       "nice_to_have": [ ... ],
       "suggested_next_actions": ["..."]
     }

Make sure at least one Must-do item addresses any payment/billing/security alert in emails, and another addresses any calendar meeting occurring within the next 2 hours relative to input/now.txt. If there are severe weather impacts that affect commute or logistics, reflect them under Should-do. Ensure digest.md and digest.json are consistent with each other.
